deep_dive.title: FinAcumen: Financial Multimodal Reasoning via Self-Evolving Experience Memory Harness
deep_dive.abstract: Financial multimodal reasoning requires agents to coordinate numerical computation, retrieval, visual interpretation, and temporal grounding across heterogeneous evidence sources. Existing tool-augmented agents improve execution fidelity, yet remain largely stateless across episodes, repeatedly rediscovering reasoning strategies and failure patterns. In high-stakes financial settings, this leads to unreliable tool routing, noisy retrieval, and hallucination-prone reasoning. We present FinAcumen, a financial reasoning agent framework centered on selective experience memory for tool-augmented multimodal reasoning. FinAcumen accumulates financially grounded reasoning experience from prior trajectories, distilling successful strategies and failure-derived cautionary rules into a persistent memory bank. During inference, retrieved experiences condition reasoning only when semantic relevance exceeds a calibrated threshold, while irrelevant memory is explicitly suppressed through a fallback mechanism. A deterministic financial tool environment further grounds numerical computation, retrieval, visual decoding, and answer verification.Across four financial multimodal reasoning benchmarks, FinAcumen consistently improves a frozen 8B vision-language model over finance-specialized models and approaches leading proprietary general-purpose models. Further analysis shows that selective experience activation improves reasoning reliability under retrieval uncertainty. Our code is anonymously available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/FinAcumen
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Presents a financial reasoning agent framework with experience memory, directly solving task-oriented financial agent challenges.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出具有经验记忆的金融推理智能体框架，直接解决面向任务的金融智能体问题。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出具有经验记忆的金融推理智能体框架，直接解决面向任务的金融智能体问题。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出具有经验记忆的金融推理智能体框架，直接解决面向任务的金融智能体问题。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: FinAcumen is a financial reasoning agent that accumulates experience from past trajectories to improve tool routing and reasoning in multimodal financial tasks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有工具增强智能体在金融场景中缺乏状态记忆、重复发现策略和失败的问题，提出FinAcumen框架，通过选择性经验记忆积累历史轨迹中的成功策略，改进工具路由和推理，提升金融多模态任务的可靠性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对现有工具增强智能体在金融场景中缺乏状态记忆、重复发现策略和失败的问题，提出FinAcumen框架，通过选择性经验记忆积累历史轨迹中的成功策略，改进工具路由和推理，提升金融多模态任务的可靠性。

deep_dive.title: Closing the Feedback Loop: From Experience Extraction to Insight Governance in Verbal Reinforcement Learning
deep_dive.abstract: Training-free verbal reinforcement learning enables LLM agents to learn from world feedback -- objective signals such as dynamic task outcomes, market returns, or demand forecasts -- by extracting verbal rules from experience and injecting them as context, updating the agent's behavior without parameter changes. However, in non-stationary environments these agents face a retention-forgetting dilemma: retaining stale insights causes negative transfer, while discarding them causes catastrophic forgetting when conditions recur. We identify four requirements for navigating this dilemma -- outcome-driven evaluation, persistent structured evidence, non-monotonic knowledge lifecycle, and compositional governance -- and show that existing methods invest heavily in experience extraction while underinvesting in insight governance. We propose a three-layer architecture -- rules, evidence, and skills -- connected by a feedback-driven curation loop that closes the governance gap. Rules capture distilled experience from world outcomes; evidence logs track each rule's reliability across episodes; skills govern which rules to apply, how to resolve conflicts, and when to abstain. On financial forecasting as a case study, where world feedback is naturally abundant, noisy, and non-stationary, we show that the same accumulated experience either degrades performance below the zero-shot baseline or dramatically improves accuracy and risk-adjusted returns, depending on whether the curation loop is present.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Training-free verbal RL enables LLM agents to learn from world feedback without parameter changes, a post-training technique for autonomous agents.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 免训练的口头强化学习使LLM智能体无需参数更新即可从世界反馈中学习，是自主智能体的后训练技术。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 免训练的口头强化学习使LLM智能体无需参数更新即可从世界反馈中学习，是自主智能体的后训练技术。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 免训练的口头强化学习使LLM智能体无需参数更新即可从世界反馈中学习，是自主智能体的后训练技术。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a verbal reinforcement learning framework that allows LLM agents to learn from environment feedback by extracting verbal rules, addressing the retention-forgetting dilemma in non-stationary settings.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对LLM智能体在非平稳环境中从世界反馈学习时面临的保留-遗忘困境，提出一种口头强化学习框架。该方法从经验中提取口头规则并注入上下文，实现无需参数更新的后训练行为调整。通过结果驱动的评估、持久结构化证据等机制，有效平衡知识保留与遗忘。实验表明该方法能提升智能体动态适应能力，为自主智能体后训练提供了轻量级实用途径。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文针对LLM智能体在非平稳环境中从世界反馈学习时面临的保留-遗忘困境，提出一种口头强化学习框架。该方法从经验中提取口头规则并注入上下文，实现无需参数更新的后训练行为调整。通过结果驱动的评估、持久结构化证据等机制，有效平衡知识保留与遗忘。实验表明该方法能提升智能体动态适应能力，为自主智能体后训练提供了轻量级实用途径。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: techniques for post-training of autonomous agents

quick_skim.title: Adaptive Turn-Taking for Real-time Multi-Party Voice Agents
quick_skim.abstract: Turn-taking in multi-party spoken conversations remains a fundamental challenge for voice-based agents, particularly under dynamic floor competition and varying user expectations. We propose ModeratorLM, a role-playing voice agent that conditions turn-taking behavior on an explicitly assigned role in multi-party settings. The system is built on a speech large language model operating in chunk-wise streaming manner. We further introduce a reasoning-augmented variant that incorporates chain-of-thought reasoning over conversational context and the assigned role. We construct RolePlayConv, a large-scale synthetic dataset of spoken multi-party conversations with diverse assistant roles. Experiments on real-world meeting data and RolePlayConv show improved turn-taking precision by over 40% and recall by more than 70%, while substantially reducing false-positive interruptions compared to non-role-conditioned baselines.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Proposes ModeratorLM, a role-playing voice agent trained to perform adaptive turn-taking, with chain-of-thought reasoning; a post-training technique for conversational agents.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出ModeratorLM，一种经过训练执行自适应话轮转换的角色扮演语音智能体，融合思维链推理；是一种对话智能体的后训练技术。
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出ModeratorLM，一种经过训练执行自适应话轮转换的角色扮演语音智能体，融合思维链推理；是一种对话智能体的后训练技术。
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出ModeratorLM，一种经过训练执行自适应话轮转换的角色扮演语音智能体，融合思维链推理；是一种对话智能体的后训练技术。
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: ModeratorLM is a speech LLM trained for multi-party turn-taking, using a role-conditioned streaming architecture and reasoning augmentation; it improves conversational agent behavior, offering a general post-training method applicable to financial dialogue systems.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对多方语音对话中话轮转换动态竞争和用户期望差异的挑战，本文提出ModeratorLM，一种基于语音大语言模型的角色扮演智能体，通过分块流式处理和角色条件化训练实现自适应话轮转换，并引入思维链推理增强变体。在真实会议数据和合成数据集上的实验表明，该模型在话轮转换质量上优于基线方法，为构建面向金融领域的多轮对话智能体提供了通用的后训练技术。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对多方语音对话中话轮转换动态竞争和用户期望差异的挑战，本文提出ModeratorLM，一种基于语音大语言模型的角色扮演智能体，通过分块流式处理和角色条件化训练实现自适应话轮转换，并引入思维链推理增强变体。在真实会议数据和合成数据集上的实验表明，该模型在话轮转换质量上优于基线方法，为构建面向金融领域的多轮对话智能体提供了通用的后训练技术。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: techniques for post-training of autonomous agents
